creator: mezz7@cox.net
text:
No, it's more like the problem is something what you have installed that  
libxul has picked up. I don't mean it's bug in what you have installed,  
which it's a bug in libxul that need to reorder the -I/usr/local stuff.  
Show us the pkg_info and I might spot what libxul doesn't like what you  
have installed stuff.

Cheers,
Mezz


--
